title: lachnagrostis elata
intro: Lachnagrostis elata, also known as tall fescue grass, is a species of perennial grass native to the temperate regions of the Northern Hemisphere. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Lachnagrostis elata include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential applications.
